channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Buyer-friendly renewals persist at 1.7 as soft market signs mount, but reinsurers signal pricing floor
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: NAIC Says Data Taken in Hack Has Been Published Online
item.description: The National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) now says the data taken earlier this month from its information technology systems has been published online by the hackers responsible. According to multiple online resources, the ShinyHunters ransomware group claimed responsibility for the NAIC breach, and allegedly stole 3.1 terabytes of data. The group said it had technology provided by the NAIC, including the System for Electronic Rate and Form Filing (SERFF), Online Premium Tax for Insurance (OPTins), Uniform Certificate Authority Application (UCAA), Enterprise Data Platform (EDP), and Regulatory Data Collection (RDC).

item.title: Guidewire expands AI capabilities for large insurance carriers
item.description: Guidewire has expanded its AI and analytics capabilities by bringing on engineers, data scientists, and federated machine learning technology from Integrate.ai to accelerate the delivery of Guidewire Intel to large property and casualty insurers. The company says the enhanced Guidewire Intel offering is designed to address several challenges facing large insurers, including gaps in state-level and specialty data, limited visibility into broader market trends, and the difficulty of modeling low-frequency, high-severity insurance events using only internal datasets. According to Guidewire, the move will enable Tier 1 and Tier 2 carriers to build predictive models using aggregated industry insights while keeping their proprietary data private and localized through federated machine learning techniques.

item.title: Fuse Launches Tape: a Free, Live Market-Intelligence Surface for the Public P&C Insurance Complex
item.description: The post Fuse Launches Tape: a Free, Live Market-Intelligence Surface for the Public P&C Insurance Complex appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . NEW YORK, NY — June 25, 2026 — Fuse International today announced the launch of Tape , a live market-intelligence surface that treats the public property-and-casualty insurance market as a single system rather than a list of individual stocks. FUSE equal-weight indices — Custom baskets for the whole complex — FUSE_ALL, FUSE_BROKER, FUSE_REINS, FUSE_ES, FUSE_WC, and FUSE_REGIONAL — rebased against SPY, IWM, and KIE so users can see how a segment is moving relative to the broad market and the insurance sector.

item.title: NHTSA Dropping Manual Brake Pedal Requirement for Fully Automated Vehicles
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration is dropping the manual brake pedal mandate for fully automated vehicles. This latest effort would eliminate the mandate for manual brake pedals in vehicles designed to be driven exclusively by automated driving systems (ADS). For ADS-equipped vehicles with manual driving controls, all existing standard requirements will be retained, according to NHTSA.

item.title: North Carolina Becomes First State to Pass Outright Ban on Litigation Financing
item.description: National insurance groups are hailing the signing of a new law in North Carolina, one that makes the state the first to place an outright ban on third-party funding of lawsuits. “North Carolina’s historic action sends a clear message that the civil justice system is not an investment vehicle.” At least 11 U.S. states have enacted some restrictions on litigation financing, including disclosure requirements, “no-influence-the-outcome” measures, and a bar on foreign interests.

item.title: Fire Tracking App Watch Duty Expands to Monitor Flood Risk
item.description: “We’re not spreading enough information fast enough on as many channels as humanly possible.” Mills built the app after his own close calls Mills founded Watch Duty in 2021 after not receiving official alerts or evacuation instructions when a fire burned near his Northern California home. A friend who did mountain rescue told Blea to download a free app called Watch Duty. Blea was one of more than 2.5 million people who used Watch Duty to track fires burning across Los Angeles County that week.

item.title: Florida Insurer Safepoint Files to Withdraw U.S. IPO Listing
item.description: Safepoint has filed to withdraw its initial public offering plans in the U.S., the Florida insurer said on Wednesday. The Tampa, Florida-based Safepoint launched its IPO last month and was expected to go public earlier this month. Florida, which used to be a difficult region for U.S.

item.title: More Than 1M People Alerted Before Northern California Quake
item.description: California’s Earthquake Early Warning System delivered advance alerts to more than one million people before the shaking began, according to Governor Gavin Newsom. The first-of-its-kind Earthquake Early Warning System provided advance notice before a 5.6-magnitude earthquake struck Mendocino County in Northern California yesterday morning. The earthquake, centered 7.5 miles east of Willits in the Mendocino Range, was felt across wide areas of Northern California just after 8 a.m., including the greater San Francisco Bay Area and Sacramento region.

item.title: Florida State Board reiterates willingness to pull-back from cat ILS if softening persists
item.description: The Florida State Board of Administration has reiterated its view that, with property catastrophe reinsurance rates softening fast catastrophe exposed insurance-linked securities (ILS) could become less attractive and the investor is even envisioning a potential “significant reduction” if the direction of travel persists. We first reported a few weeks ago that the Florida State Board of Administration, whose staff manage investment allocations to insurance-linked securities funds and reinsurance strategies on behalf of the giant Florida Retirement System Pension Plan, had said it was likely to reduce its exposure to property catastrophe risk, as expected mean loss returns have declined at a March meeting. Now, at the Florida State Board’s most recent investment advisory council meeting at the start of June, the subject was raised again by Trent Webster, Trent Webster, Senior Investment Officer.

item.title: Everest secures upsized $630m of retrocession from new Kilimanjaro III Re cat bonds
item.description: Everest Group has now priced its latest catastrophe bonds, securing the upsized $630 million of multi-peril collateralized North America focused retrocession from its new Kilimanjaro III Re Ltd. Now, we’re told that Everest Group successfully priced the six tranches of Kilimanjaro Re III cat bond notes, securing the upsized $630 million of retrocession at the lowest-ends of pricing in each case. Everest came back to the catastrophe bond market earlier in June, initially targeting at least $530 million of multi-peril collateralized retrocession from a dual issuance of three and four year tenure Kilimanjaro III Re 2026-1 and Kilimanjaro III Re 2026-2 transactions.

item.title: Walker succeeds Wilson as Tower Insurance Chief Claims Officer
item.description: New Zealand-based Tower Insurance has announced the appointment of Daniel Walker as its Chief Claims Officer. Tower Chief Executive Paul Johnston also acknowledged former Chief Claims Officer Steve Wilson, who stepped down earlier this year to return to Australia. In his new position, Walker will lead Tower’s claims function, focusing on delivering the right outcomes for customers while driving digital transformation and continuing to lift service, efficiency and performance.

item.title: Tracie Thompson appointed Global Head of Portfolio Solutions for Marsh Risk
item.description: Marsh, a risk advisory, insurance broking and reinsurance services firm, has announced the appointment of Tracie Thompson as Global Head of Portfolio Solutions, Marsh Risk, effective immediately. In her new position as Marsh, Thompson will be reporting to John Donnelly, President, Global Placement, Marsh Risk, who commented : “In today’s complex risk environment, our portfolio solutions support a fast-growing number of clients in managing their insurance and risk needs more efficiently than what they would receive in the open market. In her new position, based in New York, Thompson will lead t he continued development of Marsh’s regional and global proprietary portfolio solutions, ensuring they are scalable, market-ready, and fully address client requirements.

item.title: Goldman Sachs analysts expect reinsurance rates in Australia to fall 10-15% at July 1 renewal
item.description: Risk-adjusted reinsurance rates in Australia are projected to decline between 10-15% at the July 1st renewals, with reductions likely to be closer to the higher end of the range, according to analysts at investment bank and financial services company, Goldman Sachs. Analysts at Goldman Sachs expect favourable rate reductions for buyers at the mid-year renewals, following risk-adjusted rate declines at both the January and April renewals, with pricing returning to early 2020s levels. Despite warnings of rate reductions of up to 15% in Australia, analysts emphasise that this downward trend is unlikely to be the bottom for reinsurance rates.

item.title: Heavy Texas Rain Pushes Corpus Christi Water Emergency Into 2027
item.description: Corpus Christi, the leading crude-export hub in the US, is no longer facing an imminent emergency after heavy rain and tropical storms replenished reservoirs that had been depleted by years of drought. The South Texas coastal city had projected it could be forced to implement emergency water-saving measures by December as water supplies dwindled. But recent rainfall has helped delay the emergency date into the latter half of 2027, according to City Manager Peter Zanoni.

item.title: O’Reilly Auto Parts Sued For Disability Discrimination at Michigan Facility
item.description: O’Reilly Auto Enterprises, LLC, doing business as O’Reilly Auto Parts, a national auto parts retailer operating more than 6,500 stores throughout the United States, Puerto Rico, Mexico and Canada, violated federal law by failing to reasonably accommodate a disabled employee at its Belleville, Michigan distribution center and fired him instead, the U.S. O’Reilly Auto Enterprises, L.L.C., dba O’Reilly Auto Parts, Case No. Such alleged conduct violates the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), which prohibits discrimination based on disability.

item.title: Ship Struck in Hormuz as Oil Supertankers Turn Back Again
item.description: A few hours earlier, at least three commercial vessels, including two oil supertankers, appeared to turn around while attempting to cross Hormuz outbound on the Oman side. A ship was hit by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after several freighters turned around while attempting to cross the vital waterway, developments that may undermine what had been a rapid reopening of the energy chokepoint. The unidentified cargo ship was hit on its side and sustained damage to the bridge while it was sailing to the southeast of Oman, UK Maritime Trade Operations said.

item.title: Alan Raises €480 Million at €5.5 Billion Valuation to Accelerate AI and International Expansion
item.description: The post Alan Raises €480 Million at €5.5 Billion Valuation to Accelerate AI and International Expansion appeared first on Insurtech Insights . We’re excited to partner with Alan to accelerate its international expansion and unlock value through our ecosystem,” said Fahd Beg, Head of Investments at Prosus Group. French digital health insurer and healthcare platform Alan has raised €480 million ($550 million) in a Series G funding round, valuing the company at €5.5 billion ($6.3 billion).

item.title: Higginbotham enters South Carolina with Turbeville acquisition
item.description: Higginbotham has entered South Carolina by partnering with Turbeville Insurance Agency , an independent brokerage headquartered in Columbia with offices in Lexington, Beaufort, and Charleston. The deal marks Higginbotham’s first presence in South Carolina and gives the employee-owned brokerage an immediate statewide footprint in one of the nation’s fastest-growing states. Turbeville clients will retain their local teams while gaining access to Higginbotham’s broader capabilities, including its Day Two Services®, claims advocacy, contract review, loss control support, and specialty expertise in areas such as trucking and aviation.

item.title: Everything But The House to offer Jewelers Mutual care plans for jewelry purchases
item.description: The post Everything But The House to offer Jewelers Mutual care plans for jewelry purchases appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Everything But The House (EBTH), an online marketplace for pre-owned goods, has partnered with Jewelers Mutual to offer jewelry care plans to its customers. We are pleased to work with EBTH to provide jewelry care plan solutions that help customers protect and maintain the pieces they’ve selected, whether vintage, heirloom or contemporary.” – Mike Alexander, president of Jewelers Mutual .

item.title: MSCI to acquire First Street for $120 million
item.description: MSCI has agreed to acquire climate risk analytics company First Street for $120 million in cash, with additional payments tied to revenue targets. Expected to close in the third quarter of 2026, the deal will add First Street’s multi-hazard modeling and property-level analytics capabilities to MSCI’s climate and geospatial offerings. Founded in 2016, First Street has raised $55.5 million from investors including Congruent Ventures and General Catalyst.

item.title: NAIC Victim of Cyber Incident Via PeopleSoft System
item.description: The NAIC said the cyber incident was the result of a broad campaign that affected multiple organizations to exploit a zero-day vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft. The National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) said it was the victim of a data breach, though an investigation has found the group responsible is unlikely to have the scope of data it has claimed. NAIC uses PeopleSoft primarily for internal financial reporting purposes, it said in a June 23 update of the situation.

item.title: Homeownership Becoming Less Affordable: Kin
item.description: The homeowners insurance picture has gotten meaningfully worse in the last six months, according to Kin’s 2026 Midyear Homeownership Report . Kin surveyed 1,000 homeowners to see how sentiment has shifted since its January 2026 Homeownership Trends Report . Home Buying Decisions However, 45% of homeowners nationwide said they’ve found it difficult to find affordable insurance in the last six months—12% report it was “very difficult” with few or no options, and 33% said it was “somewhat difficult.” The share of homeowners uncertain about maintaining coverage at all has grown from 31% to 37% between December and May.

item.title: Shaping the future of the credit market – the road ahead
item.description: Speech by Alison Walters, director of consumer finance at the Credit Week: Powering the Future of Finance event. Speaker: Alison Walters, director of consumer finance Event : Credit Week: Powering the Future of Finance Delivered : 25 June 2026 Note : This is a drafted speech and may differ from the delivered version. But the world around us is changing, the credit market is changing and so are consumer demands and needs.

item.title: Why stronger Buy Now Pay Later protections are the right step for consumers
item.description: That's why we are introducing protections for consumers using BNPL . We don’t want to reduce or remove access to credit, but to ensure it is offered in a way that works for consumers. Consumers will benefit from clearer information before they borrow, proportionate affordability checks, and access to support if they get into difficulty with their repayments.

item.title: CACEIS UK censured and to pay £31.7m to WealthTek clients for weak financial crime controls
item.description: CACEIS UK, an asset servicing bank, has been censured by the FCA and will make a £31.7m voluntary payment to WealthTek clients for failing to act on information that left clients exposed to the risk of financial crime. Therese Chambers, joint executive director of enforcement and market oversight at the FCA, said: 'Strong financial crime controls keep clients’ assets safe. The FCA has now secured over £57m in total for WealthTek clients in just over a year, with action taken against CACEIS UK, Sapia Partners and Barclays Bank UK.

item.title: Cursor Deal Puts US On Track For Record Startup M&A Year
item.description: So far this year, acquirers have spent at least $119.8 billion buying private, venture-backed companies, on pace to exceed 2025’s record-setting tally. startups comes from a single deal: SpaceX ’s $60 billion acquisition of AI coding tool Cursor and its parent company Anysphere . The Cursor purchase represents the largest startup acquisition of all time, nearly double the size of the prior frontrunner, Google ’s purchase of Wiz for $32 billion.

item.title: Why modeling hurricane frequency drives better ILS investment decisions over vendor models: Schroders Capital’s Hohermuth
item.description: Benjamin Hohermuth of Schroders Capital, alongside researchers from ETH Zurich, MeteoSwiss, and Stanford University, suggests that standard vendor models used by the majority of insurance-linked securities (ILS) managers contain inherent limitations by overlooking changes in hurricane frequency. The study argues that rising catastrophe losses are heavily driven by how often storms form and make landfall, a shifting climate factor that Schroders believes is vital for making fundamentally better investment decisions for clients. At the core of the research is Schroders Capital’s in-house Schroders Capital ILS hazard model, which utilises historical hurricane data alongside climate data to create a framework for evaluating the potential changes in hurricane frequency and intensity as the climate continues to warm.

item.title: WFP highlights importance of pre‑arranged financing, including cat bonds and parametrics
item.description: Pre-arranged financing through macro-level disaster risk instruments, including catastrophe bonds and parametric insurance, are deemed a practical, scalable solution that can deliver financial support for early action to support recovery and resilience, the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) has explained. Laying out its approach to deploying Macro‑Level Disaster Risk Financing (DRF) in regions facing food security threats, including from climate and natural disasters, Lucy Bloxham and Michael Goode of the World Food Programme explain the rationale for the WFP’s use of risk transfer instruments, which comes at a highly-relevant time given its ongoing work on a food security catastrophe bond. As we recently reported, the World Food Programme (WFP) is progressing a plan under its Innovation Accelerator, to become the first to utilise catastrophe bonds for food security financing .

item.title: Aon names Mike Pasterick as North America Health Leader
item.description: Aon plc, the global professional services company specialising in risk, retirement and health solutions, has announced the appointment of Mike Pasterick as North America Health Leader, effective immediately. Pasterick will oversee Aon’s Health Solutions operations across North America, supporting clients as they respond to major developments shaping the healthcare and benefits landscape. I’m excited to work alongside our talented colleagues across North America to help clients address their most pressing health and workforce challenges.”

item.title: Janeke to lead Guy Carpenter’s combined UK P&C and London Europe team in 2027
item.description: Leon Janeke will lead the combined UK Property and Casualty and London Europe team, having previously led the London Europe team for three years. Guy Carpenter, a global risk and reinsurance specialist and a business of Marsh, is set to unite its UK Property and Casualty and London Europe teams under one leadership structure in 2027, reportedly giving clients more “seamless access” to broking, claims, analytics, and advisory expertise across Europe. Meanwhile, Dean Jenner, a market veteran with 41 years of industry experience and six years at Guy Carpenter, will assume the role of Chairman of the combined team.

item.title: Feige appointed Head of Engineering, Construction & Inland Marine Underwriting, HDI Global US
item.description: HDI Global, a corporate and specialty insurer within the Talanx Group, has appointed Dan Feige as Head of Engineering, Construction and Inland Marine Underwriting for HDI Global US. He has nearly three decades of experience leading underwriting, product, engineering, and portfolio management operations across Inland Marine, Ocean Marine, Equipment Breakdown, and Construction lines. In his new role, Feige will help advance HDI’s underwriting platform, enhancing the value the company delivers to its clients and broker partners through high-quality, technically driven, and reliable insurance solutions.

item.title: Guy Carpenter Combines UK Property and Casualty, London Europe Ops
item.description: Guy Carpenter, a business of Marsh, said it is uniting its UK Property and Casualty and London Europe teams under one leadership structure, effective at the start of 2027. Leon Janeke will lead the combined UK Property and Casualty and London Europe team, having previously led the London Europe team for three years. Topics Europe Property Property Casualty Casualty London

item.title: Thousands Feared Dead After Two Major Earthquakes Hit Venezuela
item.description: Thousands of Venezuelans were feared dead on Thursday after two powerful earthquakes wreaked havoc in and around the capital Caracas, trapping people beneath the rubble of collapsed buildings and setting off powerful aftershocks. Emergency workers scrambled over the debris of collapsed buildings in Caracas as night fell, while distraught relatives sought help for loved ones feared trapped. But from that building, I only saw that one family got out.” Website Shows 10,000 People Unaccounted For Interim President Delcy Rodriguez said at least 164 people were confirmed dead and nearly 1,000 injured, and the worst-affected area was La Guaira state, near Caracas and home to the city’s airport.

item.title: Florida-Based Safepoint Withdraws IPO Just as it Was Expected to Launch
item.description: Florida-based Safepoint Holdings, parent company of Safepoint Insurance and Manatee Insurance Exchanged has filed to withdraw its initial public offering plans in the U.S., the Florida insurer said on Wednesday. The Tampa-based Safepoint launched its IPO last month and was expected to go public earlier this month. Safepoint Insurance reported just 1,020 policies in force in Florida at the end of 2025, according to quarterly reports to Florida regulators.

item.title: RT Specialty Names Brendan Mulshine CEO in Planned Leadership Succession
item.description: RT Specialty, the wholesale brokerage division of Ryan Specialty, has announced a planned leadership transition, appointing Brendan Mulshine as Chief Executive Officer effective August 1, 2026. Experienced Internal Leader Takes the Helm Mulshine joined Ryan Specialty in 2012 and has held leadership roles across the company’s business units, giving him broad operational experience throughout the organization. Leadership Changes Across Property and Casualty Practices Alongside the CEO succession, RT Specialty announced a series of senior leadership appointments across its property and casualty divisions.

item.title: Taktile Raises $110 Million Series C to Accelerate AI-Powered Financial Decision Automation
item.description: The post Taktile Raises $110 Million Series C to Accelerate AI-Powered Financial Decision Automation appeared first on Insurtech Insights . AI decision automation platform Taktile has raised $110 million in a Series C funding round led by Goldman Sachs, with participation from Tiger Global, Index Ventures and Y Combinator, as financial institutions accelerate the adoption of artificial intelligence for high-stakes operational decisions. The Berlin-based company develops AI-powered decision automation software for banks, insurers and fintechs, enabling organizations to automate complex workflows including insurance claims, fraud investigations, customer onboarding and credit decisions while maintaining governance and human oversight.

item.title: Federato Expands Into Asia-Pacific and Japan With AI-Native Insurance Platform
item.description: AI insurance platform Federato has expanded into Asia-Pacific and Japan, launching regional operations with a new Sydney office and appointing former Guidewire executive Roland Slee as Regional Vice President for Asia Pacific and Japan (APJ). The expansion brings Federato’s AI-native insurance platform to commercial insurers and managing general agents (MGAs) across the region, initially targeting Australia and New Zealand before expanding into Singapore, Japan and South Korea. The carriers and MGAs I’ve spoken with here are sophisticated, well-capitalised, and eager to explore the improved business outcomes Federato’s AI-native platform has delivered in the rest of the world.” Former Guidewire Executive to Lead APJ Growth To support its regional expansion, Federato has appointed Slee to lead operations across Asia-Pacific and Japan.

item.title: 🦄 Alan raises €480 million at a €5.5 billion valuation
item.description: French health insurer and healthcare platform Alan has raised €480 million ($550 million) in a Series G financing round that values the company at €5.5 billion ($6.3 billion). Founded in 2016, Alan has evolved from a digital health insurer into what it calls a “prevention insurance” company, combining health coverage, care navigation, wellbeing services, and AI-powered health assistance in a single platform. The company said it surpassed €800 million in annual recurring revenue in the first quarter of 2026, up 53% year over year, serves more than 1.1 million members, and is profitable in France.

item.title: Hyperexponential deploys across Allianz Commercial’s core lines
item.description: Pricing and underwriting platform Hyperexponential has been deployed across Allianz Commercial’s priority lines of business as part of a multi-year pricing and underwriting transformation. Working with Allianz Commercial, hx has already delivered 13 pricing tools in 13 weeks, strengthening pricing tool coverage across core lines of business. Delivering 13 tools in 13 weeks demonstrates what’s possible when you build on modern infrastructure designed for actuarial workflows.” – Tim Pitt, Chief Pricing and Portfolio Officer at Allianz Commercial.

item.title: King Risk Partners acquires Perry Insurance
item.description: King Risk Partners has acquired Perry Insurance Agency, an independent insurance agency based in North Andover, Massachusetts. Headquartered in Gainesville, Florida, King Risk Partners is the 51st-largest insurance brokerage in the US and has been expanding through a combination of organic growth and acquisitions. This partnership supports our continued expansion in Massachusetts and reflects our disciplined approach to growth, which is focused on aligning with agencies that share our values and strengthen the solutions we deliver to clients.” – Scott Popilek, Chief Executive Officer of King Risk Partners.

item.title: «El avance hacia la economía de la confianza pasa por un liderazgo en valores»
item.description: «El avance hacia la economía de la confianza pasa por un liderazgo en valores»

item.title: Así impacta el envejecimiento en el mercado laboral
item.description: Fundación Mapfre ha celebrado un encuentro en el que ha presentado el libro ‘El futuro del trabajo es gris’, del experto en liderazgo Dan Pontefract, impulsado en España por la fundación y publicado por Almuzara.

item.title: Regulación, confianza y competitividad: un equilibrio necesario desde la visión bancoaseguradora
item.description: La semana pasada EIOPA publicó una reflexión sobre la competitividad y la supervisión que merece un reconocimiento explícito: por la claridad de sus argumentos y también por el valor de una contribución en un momento en el que Europa afronta retos de enorme calado.

item.title: A Fire Tracking App Used by Millions Expands to Help Monitor Dangerous Floods
item.description: When a fire broke out a few miles from his Altadena, California, home the evening of Jan. On the app, he could see the fire’s perimeter, track evacuation orders and read updates about the emergency response. “It influenced me to leave the home sooner than later,” said Blea, who left with his wife and son that evening, before the Eaton Fire destroyed their home.

item.title: More Americans Are Moving Away From Flood Risk Than Toward It
item.description: Americans are leaving some of the nation’s most flood-prone communities at an accelerating pace, suggesting that climate concerns are beginning to influence migration decisions alongside more traditional factors such as housing costs and quality of life. Florida’s Miami-Dade County saw the largest net domestic outflow among flood-prone counties, losing more than 72,000 residents, the biggest loss on record for the county. More than half of high-risk counties analyzed still recorded net inflows, particularly in relatively affordable parts of Florida, Texas and North Carolina.

item.title: Montgomery v. Caribe Increases Litigation Activity, Not Shipper Exposure
item.description: For insurance carriers, however, especially those insuring shippers, the more important question to be considered is not what Montgomery says about broker liability, but what it does to upstream exposure. The background of the case shows that petitioner Shawn Montgomery sustained severe injuries after his tractor trailer was struck by a truck driven by respondent Yosniel Varela-Mojena, who was driving a load of plastic pots through Illinois for motor carrier Caribe Transport II LLC. Instead, exposure has always turned on traditional tort concepts—namely duty, control, agency, and causation, and Montgomery leaves that framework entirely intact.

item.title: What Happens to Property Pricing in ’27, Insurance, Reinsurance Execs Ask
item.description: Williamson prefaced his question about what happens in 2027 by first confirming that property pricing in the reinsurance industry and the underlying primary industry has been heading down this year. “Do we see, which I’m hopeful we will, a discipline that creates a bit of a floor,” Williamson asked, going one step beyond answering a question about the adequacy of property reinsurance pricing posed by Taoufik Gharib, director and lead analyst for S&P Global Ratings, during an “Executive Perspectives” session of the S&P conference. Insurance and reinsurance executives speaking at a recent industry conference said there were few surprises during midyear 2026 reinsurance renewals—with past overreactions on price hikes explaining this year’s declines.

item.title: MS Amlin commits US$50m to Natural Disaster Fund to aid Global South
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon’s Reinsurance Solutions names Chin Fong Teh as APAC chief of staff
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles
